title: Legislation and Regulation
shortSummary: Learn how policy becomes binding law in Australia, how delegated legislation works, and why the Senate often decides whether governments can actually deliver.
whyItMatters: Laws are how political promises become durable obligations. If you do not understand the legislative funnel, you cannot tell the difference between announcement, policy intention, and actual legal change.
coreConcepts: Government bills and private members' bills
First reading, second reading, committee stages, third reading
mentalModels: Legislation is a funnel, not a single vote
The Senate is often the realism chamber
A law without implementation capacity stays symbolic

goDeeper.label: Draft a private member's bill
goDeeper.description: Use a project to test whether you can move from principle into legal structure.

goDeeper.label: Continue into influence and lobbying
goDeeper.description: See how actors try to move policy and law before the public vote arrives.
